title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Panamax Canal Congestion, Handysize
Brazilian iron ore export volumes increased in August, leading to a boost in Capesize tonne days
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/congestion-report-dry-download/2pd9h1/346894656?h=8acfMf1qc-u79gjN59v5dhODYRSXC2mM55w_pFT8OPs
In the third week of September, freight rates in the various ship categories increased significantly, indicating a robust upward trend. At the same time, there is a noticeable optimism in Chinese economic activity, supported by a number of strategic stimulus measures by policy makers. In recent weeks, policymakers in China have introduced a comprehensive package of measures to boost economic growth and support the real estate market and the national currency.

children.title: Chart of the Week: Panamax Canal Congestion, Handysize
section_path: Chart of the Week: Panamax Canal Congestion, Handysize
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Panamax Canal Congestion, Handysize
children.summary:
Brazilian iron ore export volumes increased in August, leading to a boost in Capesize tonne days
Image reference: assets/2023-09-20_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-20t122056953_2914ef495a43.png
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/congestion-report-dry-download/2pd9h1/346894656?h=8acfMf1qc-u79gjN59v5dhODYRSXC2mM55w_pFT8OPs
In the third week of September, freight rates in the various ship categories increased significantly, indicating a robust upward trend. At the same time, there is a noticeable optimism in Chinese economic activity, supported by a number of strategic stimulus measures by policy makers.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes Market Rates ($/t) Firmer
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-20T122205.675.png | assets/2023-09-20_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-20t122205675_aa1f1d52f40c.png
In the third week of September, rates recovered and sentiment remained positive across all ship size categories, with the recovery particularly pronounced in the Panamax and Handysize segments. Capesize vessel freight rates from Brazil to North China exceeded $18/tonne, representing a 5% w-o-w increase.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (# vessels) Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-20T122606.985.png | assets/2023-09-20_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-20t122606985_f73bfe0774af.png
The number of ballast ships remained at a higher level in the smaller ship categories, while a downward trend was observed in the Capesize and Panamax segments. Capesize SE Africa: The number of ballasters dropped to 106, which is ten less than three weeks ago and 14% less than the peak recorded almost ten weeks ago.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND
children.summary:
Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size Tonne Days Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-20T122758.378.png | assets/2023-09-20_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-20t122758378_3636da5fc678.png
The prevailing sentiment is downward, although there were signs of a possible recovery in the Capesize segment. Capesize: Demand growth in tonnes per day again fell to lower levels than the previous two weeks, but remained stronger than the lows recorded five weeks ago.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports No of Vessels Increasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-20T123024.179.png | assets/2023-09-20_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-20t123024179_ba22fade2abb.png
The increase seems to continue in the larger ship categories, while there is evidence of a decline in the Supramax segment. Capesize : The number of congested ships was 116, 16 more than two weeks ago.
